experiment: GATE 1c -- realistic API-heavy token->API precision (allowlist ON)
allowlist: synthetic SA + real operators: kube-state-metrics, coredns, metrics-server, traefik, local-path-provisioner
verdict: Allowlist LOGIC is precise: 515/515 RESOLVED benign API clients (incl. the real kube-state-metrics/traefik/coredns/metrics-server operators) suppressed, 0 mis-fires; attacker 12/12 detected. Residual 27 FP (4.98%, 54/hr) are ALL resolver cold-start (unresolved pod at first API call, scales with pod-churn) -- the bounded cold-cache class (cf. E5), closable by warming the pod->SA map on pod-create. Honest read: identity-scoping makes the allowlist decision precise; production-grade precision additionally needs to eliminate the cold-start window.
churn_restarts_during_run: aggressive (~1 pod restart / 180s) to stress the cold-start window
